fault_hypothesis: On line 67, the overlap condition uses `<=` and `>=` instead of `<` and `>`, causing touching intervals (where end == s or start == e) to be incorrectly treated as non-overlapping.

spec.expr: not (end__1 <= s or start__1 >= e) == not (end__1 < s or start__1 > e) or (end__1 != s and start__1 != e)

constraints.intent: The boundary condition using <= and >= differs from < and > exactly when end==s or start==e; this constraint is FALSE on failing tests where touching intervals are incorrectly accepted.

spec.expr: not (end__1 < s or start__1 > e) == not (end__1 <= s or start__1 >= e) or (end__1 != s and start__1 != e)

constraints.intent: At the end of each loop iteration, the strict and non-strict overlap checks should agree unless intervals touch exactly at a boundary point.
Verify that end is strictly greater than start before the overlap check uses these values.

spec.expr: __cbfl_result[0] is False or all(not (interval[1] < s or interval[0] > e) == False for s, e in existing)

constraints.intent: If insertion succeeded, no existing interval should touch or overlap the new interval under the strict boundary definition.
